_doc: Declarative IAM S3-access contract for alpha-engine-evaluator (config#1404).
Single source of truth for every top-level s3://alpha-engine-research/<prefix>/
namespace the evaluator's grading + director code reads or writes, and the
access mode it needs. This file is the CODE-SIDE half of a cross-repo contract:
* THIS repo (crucible-evaluator) declares the S3 access surface here and a CI
coverage test (tests/test_iam_s3_contract.py) fails any PR that grows the
code's S3-access surface without updating this contract.
* The OPS repo (nous-ergon-ops) holds the deployed grant document
alpha-engine-evaluator/infrastructure/iam/alpha-engine-evaluator-role/
alpha-engine-evaluator-policy.json and verifies (check-iam-code-coverage.py)
that the live policy grants exactly the prefixes declared here.
Why this exists: a new cross-module artifact prefix could enter producer/consumer
code with no matching role grant, surfacing only as a confusing AccessDenied at
the grading canary (403-not-404 for a missing key when s3:ListBucket lacks the
prefix) -- e.g. _substrate/deploy_success.json in PR #75 (Deploy red on main).
The pre-existing live-vs-codified IAM drift check could not catch it because the
codified policy ALSO lacked the prefix (they agreed). The missing dimension is
code-referenced-prefix -> codified-grant coverage; this contract closes it.
access modes:
read      -> s3:ListBucket (prefix condition) + s3:GetObject on <prefix>/*
readwrite -> the above + s3:PutObject on <prefix>/* (the code writes here)
